channel.title: DysonX Public Signals
channel.description: Source-attributed public Signals tracking AI and AGI intelligence.

item.title: Agentic AI adoption accelerates from Codex usage evidence
item.description: A June 2026 research paper uses Codex usage data as evidence that agentic AI is moving from developer tooling toward broader organizational workflows.

item.title: AI Agent Index highlights transparency gaps in deployed agents
item.description: The 2025 AI Agent Index documents technical and safety features across deployed agentic AI systems and flags uneven transparency among developers.

item.title: EU AI Office becomes the GPAI governance surface to watch
item.description: The European AI Office supports AI Act implementation, especially general-purpose AI rules, evaluations, guidance, and enforcement workflows.

item.title: METR time-horizon work reframes autonomous capability tracking
item.description: METR tracks frontier AI progress through task-completion time horizons, measuring the length of tasks AI agents can complete at defined success rates.

item.title: NIST AI governance is anchored in standards
item.description: NIST frames AI governance around measurement, risk management, standards, guidelines, benchmarks, and evaluations.

item.title: Separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
item.description: A new analysis from OpenAI reveals issues in SWE-Bench Pro, a popular coding benchmark, raising concerns about reliability and accuracy in evaluating AI models.

item.title: How agents are transforming work
item.description: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

item.title: ARCANA: A Reflective Multi-Agent Program Synthesis Framework for ARC-AGI-2 Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2607.09059v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present ARCANA, a collaborative multi agent framework for solving ARC AGI 2 tasks under strict test time and hardware constraints. ARCANA decomposes each task into iterative perception,...

item.title: Dec-MARVEL: Decentralized Multi-Agent Exploration without Communication under Budget Constraints
item.description: arXiv:2607.09060v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-UAV exploration is often constrained by unreliable communication, limited field-of-view sensing (e.g., lightweight onboard camera), and finite travel budgets that require each robot t...

item.title: Fictional Worldbuilding: Multi-Agent LLM Collaboration with Hierarchical Context Compression and Iterative Review
item.description: arXiv:2607.09403v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Worldbuilding, the construction of coherent fictional worlds, is a foundational task in game design and literary creation. Large Language Models (LLMs) offer new possibilities for automated...

item.title: HERO: A Heterogeneity-Aware Benchmark Library for Federated Continual Learning
item.description: arXiv:2607.08784v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated continual learning (FCL) evaluates how distributed clients learn from changing data streams while retaining previously learned knowledge. Existing evaluations are difficult to com...

item.title: Interval Certifications for Multilayered Perceptrons via Lattice Traversal
item.description: arXiv:2607.08773v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this work we present a rigorous theoretical framework to a foundational problem of AI safety, namely adversarial robustness. In particular, we show that the adversarial robustness proble...

item.title: KV-PRM: Efficient Process Reward Modeling via KV-Cache Transfer for Multi-Agent Test-Time Scaling
item.description: arXiv:2607.09153v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Process Reward Models (PRMs) have been proven to be highly effective in guiding test-time scaling (TTS) methods, which significantly boost the capabilities of LLM-based multi-agent systems....

item.title: Long-Horizon-Terminal-Bench: Testing the Limits of Agents on Long-Horizon Terminal Tasks with Dense Reward-Based Grading
item.description: arXiv:2607.08964v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents have become capable of autonomously completing short, well-specified tasks. However, existing terminal benchmarks largely focus on simple problems that finish within minutes and a...

item.title: Neuro-Agentic Control: A Deep Learning-based LLM-Powered Agentic AI Framework for Controlling Security Controls
item.description: arXiv:2607.09076v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cyberattacks on operational technology are increasingly causing costly downtime and physical damage, exposing the limitations of traditional rule-based monitoring in industrial IoT environm...

item.title: OpenProver: Agentic and Interactive Theorem Proving with Lean 4
item.description: arXiv:2607.09217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this system paper, we present OpenProver, an open-source system for LLM-driven automated theorem proving (ATP) with integrated Lean 4 formal verification. OpenProver integrates a Planner...

item.title: ProofCouncil: An LLM Agent for Solving Open Mathematical Problems
item.description: arXiv:2607.09474v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown increasing promise in solving open problems in mathematics. However, their performance can be further improved through agentic workflows tailored to...

item.title: Scoped Verification for Reliable Long-Horizon Agentic Context Evolution under Distribution Shift
item.description: arXiv:2607.09175v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deployed LLM agents rely on agentic context, the model-external textual control content assembled by an operational harness. In this work, the mutable component of that context is a persist...

item.title: Toward Auditable AI Scientists: A Hypothesis Evolution Protocol for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.09195v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly expected to play a central role in AI-driven scientific discovery. Equipped with broad knowledge, flexible reasoning, and tool use, they h...

item.title: Feedback Manipulation Regularization: Enabling Offline Agent Alignment for Imitation Learning
item.description: arXiv:2607.07859v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) research has increasingly shifted focus towards alignment, ensuring agents learn behaviors adhering to human values. While human demonstrations and feedback have...

item.title: From Prompts to Contracts: Harness Engineering for Auditable Enterprise LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.08028v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enterprise large language model (LLM) applications often begin as prototypes whose behavior is carried by prompts and retrieval context. Productization adds requirements for source boundari...

item.title: Persuasion Attacks Can Decrease Effectiveness of CoT Monitoring
item.description: arXiv:2607.08066v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) monitoring is a promising safety mechanism for AI agents, based on the premise that visible reasoning traces can surface misaligned or deceptive behavior. While effec...

item.title: A Reliability Assessment of LALM Audio Judges for Full-Duplex Voice Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.07985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We report the empirical reliability of Gemini models as audio judges that score full-duplex agent conversations directly from the raw stereo waveform, tested across three models in the Gemi...

item.title: A Transdiagnostic Space of Disorder Like Phenotypes in Reinforcement Learning Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.07753v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modelling psychological disorders in artificial agents offers both a testbed for computational psychiatry and a lens on the failure modes of affective control. Prior work induces one or two...

item.title: Harness VLA: Steering Frozen VLAs into Reliable Manipulation Primitives via Memory-Guided Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.08448v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language-conditioned manipulation requires both precise contact-rich control and robust reasoning over language, scenes, and long horizons. End-to-end Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models pr...

item.title: Jet-Long: Efficient Long-Context Extension with Dynamic Bifocal RoPE
item.description: arXiv:2607.07740v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern LLMs are increasingly deployed in long-context applications such as retrieval-augmented generation, repository-level coding, and agentic workflows whose accumulated reasoning and too...

item.title: MASTE: A Multi-Agent Pipeline for Zero-Shot Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction
item.description: arXiv:2607.08080v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction (ASTE) requires jointly identifying (aspect, opinion, sentiment) triples from a given review sentence. While large language models (LLMs) achieve strong...

item.title: Tool-Making and Self-Evolving LLM Agents in Low-Latency Systems
item.description: arXiv:2607.08010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Production LLM agents often waste latency and reliability by regenerating code for the same procedural steps on every request. We replace this inference-time coding loop with an agentic too...

item.title: A Closed-Loop Multi-Agent Framework for Robust Multi-Robot Manipulation
item.description: arXiv:2607.06990v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-robot systems provide the parallelism and redundancy necessary for long-horizon tasks, while Large Language Models (LLMs) offer the reasoning capabilities to decompose these objective...

item.title: AgentLens: Production-Assessed Trajectory Reviews for Coding Agent Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2607.06624v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present AgentLens, a production-assessed benchmark for interactive code agents. Most code-agent benchmarks reduce a run to a single bit -- did the task pass? -- but the people who actual...
